articles.title: Umpire admits he forgot the count during bizarre Red Sox sequence
articles.description:
“I’ve never done that before. I’m not happy about it. Just made a mistake.”

The post Umpire admits he forgot the count during bizarre Red Sox sequence appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Umpire mistakenly forgot the count during a Red Sox game on April 1, 2026.
This incident highlights the pressure umpires face in high-stakes games.
Local fans express frustration over the impact of umpire errors on game outcomes.

articles.title: Federal judge in Mass. rules Trump unlawfully revoked status of migrants who used entry app
articles.description:
Last April, the Trump administration instructed as many as 900,000 migrants who had used the app to leave “immediately.”

The post Federal judge in Mass. rules Trump unlawfully revoked status of migrants who used entry app appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Federal judge rules Trump unlawfully revoked migrant status for app users in Massachusetts.
Approximately 900,000 migrants were affected by the April 2020 directive.
Boston residents may see increased stability for local immigrant communities following the ruling.

articles.title: Hershey says it will shift back to classic recipe for all Reese’s products after criticism
articles.description:
The Hershey, Pennsylvania-based company said it will also be making other changes to its sweets portfolio next year, including transitioning to natural colors and enhancing Kit-Kat’s recipe to make it creamier.

The post Hershey says it will shift back to classic recipe for all Reese’s products after criticism appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Hershey will revert to classic recipes for all Reese's products by 2024.
The company will also enhance Kit-Kat's recipe for a creamier texture.
Boston residents can expect improved product quality and natural colors in their favorite candies.

articles.title: Hudson man accused of beating grandmother to death with skateboard, also injuring father and sister
articles.description:
Authorities don’t yet know the circumstances leading up to the alleged attack, Middlesex District Attorney Marian Ryan said.

The post Hudson man accused of beating grandmother to death with skateboard, also injuring father and sister appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Hudson man allegedly killed grandmother, injured father and sister with skateboard on March 31.
Middlesex District Attorney Marian Ryan is investigating the circumstances of the attack.
Local residents are concerned about safety following this violent incident in the community.
